users.title: Get the authenticated user
users.descriptionHTML: OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the user scope in order for the response to include private profile information.

response.description: Response with public and private profile information

users.title: Update the authenticated user

bodyParameters.name: name
bodyParameters.description: The new name of the user.

bodyParameters.name: email
bodyParameters.description: The publicly visible email address of the user.

bodyParameters.name: blog
bodyParameters.description: The new blog URL of the user.

bodyParameters.name: twitter_username
bodyParameters.description: The new Twitter username of the user.

bodyParameters.name: company
bodyParameters.description: The new company of the user.

bodyParameters.name: location
bodyParameters.description: The new location of the user.

bodyParameters.name: hireable
bodyParameters.description: The new hiring availability of the user.

bodyParameters.name: bio
bodyParameters.description: The new short biography of the user.

users.descriptionHTML: Note: If your email is set to private and you send an email parameter as part of this request to update your profile, your privacy settings are still enforced: the email address will not be displayed on your public profile or via the API.

request.description: Example of updating blog and name

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

users.title: Get a user using their ID

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: account_id parameter

users.descriptionHTML:
Provides publicly available information about someone with a GitHub account. This method takes their durable user ID instead of their login, which can change over time.

If you are requesting information about an Enterprise Managed User, or a GitHub App bot that is installed in an organization that uses Enterprise Managed Users, your requests must be authenticated as a user or GitHub App that has access to the organization to view that account's information. If you are not authorized, the request will return a 404 Not Found status.

The email key in the following response is the publicly visible email address from your GitHub Enterprise Cloud profile page. When setting up your profile, you can select a primary email address to be public which provides an email entry for this endpoint. If you do not set a public email address for email, then it will have a value of null. You only see publicly visible email addresses when authenticated with GitHub Enterprise Cloud. For more information, see Authentication.

The Emails API enables you to list all of your email addresses, and toggle a primary email to be visible publicly. For more information, see Emails API.

users.title: List users

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: A user ID. Only return users with an ID greater than this ID.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

users.descriptionHTML:
Lists all users, in the order that they signed up on GitHub Enterprise Cloud. This list includes personal user accounts and organization accounts.

Note: Pagination is powered exclusively by the since parameter. Use the Link header to get the URL for the next page of users.

users.title: Get a user

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

users.descriptionHTML:
Provides publicly available information about someone with a GitHub account.

If you are requesting information about an Enterprise Managed User, or a GitHub App bot that is installed in an organization that uses Enterprise Managed Users, your requests must be authenticated as a user or GitHub App that has access to the organization to view that account's information. If you are not authorized, the request will return a 404 Not Found status.

The email key in the following response is the publicly visible email address from your GitHub Enterprise Cloud profile page. When setting up your profile, you can select a primary email address to be public which provides an email entry for this endpoint. If you do not set a public email address for email, then it will have a value of null. You only see publicly visible email addresses when authenticated with GitHub Enterprise Cloud. For more information, see Authentication.

The Emails API enables you to list all of your email addresses, and toggle a primary email to be visible publicly. For more information, see Emails API.

users.title: Get contextual information for a user

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

parameters.name: subject_type
parameters.description: Identifies which additional information you'd like to receive about the person's hovercard. Can be organization, repository, issue, pull_request. Required when using subject_id.

parameters.name: subject_id
parameters.description: Uses the ID for the subject_type you specified. Required when using subject_type.

users.descriptionHTML:
Provides hovercard information. You can find out more about someone in relation to their pull requests, issues, repositories, and organizations.

The subject_type and subject_id parameters provide context for the person's hovercard, which returns more information than without the parameters. For example, if you wanted to find out more about octocat who owns the Spoon-Knife repository, you would use a subject_type value of repository and a subject_id value of 1300192 (the ID of the Spoon-Knife repository).

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

attestations.title: List attestations by bulk subject digests

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

bodyParameters.name: subject_digests
bodyParameters.description: List of subject digests to fetch attestations for.

bodyParameters.name: predicate_type
bodyParameters.description: Optional filter for fetching attestations with a given predicate type. This option accepts provenance, sbom, release, or freeform text for custom predicate types.

attestations.descriptionHTML:
List a collection of artifact attestations associated with any entry in a list of subject digests owned by a user.

The collection of attestations returned by this endpoint is filtered according to the authenticated user's permissions; if the authenticated user cannot read a repository, the attestations associated with that repository will not be included in the response. In addition, when using a fine-grained access token the attestations:read permission is required.

Please note: in order to offer meaningful security benefits, an attestation's signature and timestamps must be cryptographically verified, and the identity of the attestation signer must be validated. Attestations can be verified using the GitHub CLI attestation verify command. For more information, see our guide on how to use artifact attestations to establish a build's provenance.

attestations_subject_digests.description: Mapping of subject digest to bundles.

has_next.description: Indicates whether there is a next page.

has_previous.description: Indicates whether there is a previous page.

next.description: The cursor to the next page.

previous.description: The cursor to the previous page.

parameters.name: username

bodyParameters.name: subject_digests
bodyParameters.description: List of subject digests associated with the artifact attestations to delete.

attestations.descriptionHTML: Delete artifact attestations in bulk by either subject digests or unique ID.

attestations.title: List attestations

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

parameters.name: subject_digest
parameters.description: Subject Digest

parameters.name: predicate_type
parameters.description: Optional filter for fetching attestations with a given predicate type. This option accepts provenance, sbom, release, or freeform text for custom predicate types.

attestations.descriptionHTML:
List a collection of artifact attestations with a given subject digest that are associated with repositories owned by a user.

The collection of attestations returned by this endpoint is filtered according to the authenticated user's permissions; if the authenticated user cannot read a repository, the attestations associated with that repository will not be included in the response. In addition, when using a fine-grained access token the attestations:read permission is required.

Please note: in order to offer meaningful security benefits, an attestation's signature and timestamps must be cryptographically verified, and the identity of the attestation signer must be validated. Attestations can be verified using the GitHub CLI attestation verify command. For more information, see our guide on how to use artifact attestations to establish a build's provenance.

bundle.description:
The attestation's Sigstore Bundle.
Refer to the [Sigstore Bundle Specification](https://github.com/sigstore/protobuf-specs/blob/main/protos/sigstore_bundle.proto) for more information.

gpg-keys.title: Delete a GPG key for the authenticated user

parameters.name: gpg_key_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the GPG key.

gpg-keys.descriptionHTML:
Removes a GPG key from the authenticated user's GitHub account.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:gpg_key scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

gpg-keys.title: List GPG keys for a user

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

gpg-keys.descriptionHTML: Lists the GPG keys for a user. This information is accessible by anyone.

keys.title: List public SSH keys for the authenticated user

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

keys.descriptionHTML:
Lists the public SSH keys for the authenticated user's GitHub account.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:public_key scope to use this endpoint.

keys.title: Create a public SSH key for the authenticated user

bodyParameters.name: title
bodyParameters.description: A descriptive name for the new key.

bodyParameters.name: key
bodyParameters.description: The public SSH key to add to your GitHub account.

keys.descriptionHTML:
Adds a public SSH key to the authenticated user's GitHub account.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:public_key scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

keys.title: Get a public SSH key for the authenticated user

parameters.name: key_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the key.

keys.descriptionHTML:
View extended details for a single public SSH key.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:public_key scope to use this endpoint.

keys.title: Delete a public SSH key for the authenticated user

parameters.name: key_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the key.

keys.descriptionHTML:
Removes a public SSH key from the authenticated user's GitHub account.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:public_key scope to use this endpoint.

keys.title: List public keys for a user

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

keys.descriptionHTML: Lists the verified public SSH keys for a user. This is accessible by anyone.

social-accounts.title: List social accounts for the authenticated user

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

social-accounts.title: Add social accounts for the authenticated user

bodyParameters.name: account_urls
bodyParameters.description: Full URLs for the social media profiles to add.

social-accounts.descriptionHTML:
Add one or more social accounts to the authenticated user's profile.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the user scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

social-accounts.title: Delete social accounts for the authenticated user

bodyParameters.name: account_urls
bodyParameters.description: Full URLs for the social media profiles to delete.

social-accounts.descriptionHTML:
Deletes one or more social accounts from the authenticated user's profile.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the user scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

social-accounts.descriptionHTML: Lists social media accounts for a user. This endpoint is accessible by anyone.
